verse_text: And He charged him to tell no one, 'But go and show yourself to the priest, and make an offering for your cleansing, as atestimony to them, just as Moses commanded.'

links.context: The biblical law concerning leprosy is found in Lv 13; 14. [[DEU.24:8]] describes the purification of lepers and leprous houses, which was a duty entrusted to the priests. Leprosy was considered a direct punishment for sins, and as lepers were unclean, they were not permitted to live in the community or to worship in synagogues or the temple. Touching the unclean was forbidden (Lv 7:21), yet Jesus touched the leper, showing His compassion, and showing that He is not subject to the Law but over it. To the clean, nothing is unclean. (See also note at [[LUK.5:14]].)
On the cleansing of the leper, see notes at [[MAT.8:1]]-4 and [[LUK.5:12]]-16.
